UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, derived from their resume and editable by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

summary.description: A brief summary or personal statement.

slug.description: The URL-friendly slug for the user's profile page.

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Represents a user's work experience entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

startDate.description: The start date of the employment.

endDate.description: The end date of the employment (or null if current).

description.description: A description of the responsibilities and achievements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

institution.description: The name of the educational institution.

startDate.description: The start date of the education.

endDate.description: The end date of the education.

description.description: A description of the studies and achievements.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a skill possessed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

level.description: The user's proficiency level in the skill.

Theme.title: Theme
Theme.description: Represents a visual theme that can be applied to a user's profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Theme entity.

primaryColor.description: The primary color of the theme (e.g., hex code).

secondaryColor.description: The secondary color of the theme.

fontFamily.description: The font family used in the theme.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.  Note: Authentication data (passwords) are assumed to be handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

definition.description: Stores user authentication and profile information. It only store authentication information, profile data in UserProfile entity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores user profile data.  Owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores work experience entries for a specific user. Owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: workExperienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the work experience entry.

definition.description: Stores education entries for a specific user. Owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: educationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the education entry.

definition.description: Stores skill entries for a specific user. Owned by the user identified by userId.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill entry.

definition.description: Stores available themes for user profiles.

params.name: themeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the theme.

definition.description: Stores slugs for user profiles, enforcing uniqueness.  This is managed by a Cloud Function to maintain consistency with /users/{userId}/userProfile.  Data is duplicated here for efficient slug lookup.

params.name: slug
params.description: The unique slug for the user profile.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for the ResumeRack application. It prioritizes authorization independence and clear security rules. User profiles, work experience, education, and skills are organized under each user's document to maintain data locality and simplify authorization. The Theme entity is stored in a top-level collection, and each user profile references one.

Key aspects of the design include:

1.  **Authorization Independence:** UserProfile and all its subcollections (WorkExperience, Education, Skill) are located under `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring authorization is based solely on `request.auth.uid` and data within the user's document.
2.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection serves a distinct purpose, ensuring a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents within `/users/{userId}/workExperiences` have the same access control requirements (accessible only by the user).
3.  **Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is consistently used for user-owned data (UserProfile, WorkExperience, Education, Skill). Themes are global and accessible by anyone, so they are in a top-level collection.
4.  **QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure supports secure `list` operations. Because the data is nested under `/users/{userId}`, listing operations are always scoped to the authenticated user.
5. **Slug Uniqueness:**  The `/userProfilesBySlug/{slug}` collection is used to quickly check for uniqueness of the chosen slug.  Writing to this collection is done via a Cloud Function that enforces uniqueness and consistency with the main `/users/{userId}/userProfile` document.

This design ensures data integrity, simplifies security rules, and enhances the scalability and maintainability of the ResumeRack application.
